speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Com’st thou with deep premeditated lines,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: With written pamphlets studiously devised?

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Humphrey of Gloucester, if thou canst accuse

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Or aught intend’st to lay unto my charge,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Do it without invention, suddenly,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: As I with sudden and extemporal speech

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Purpose to answer what thou canst object.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Presumptuous priest, this place commands my patience,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Or thou shouldst find thou hast dishonored me.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Think not, although in writing I preferred

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The manner of thy vile outrageous crimes,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That therefore I have forged or am not able

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Verbatim to rehearse the method of my pen.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: No, prelate, such is thy audacious wickedness,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thy lewd, pestiferous, and dissentious pranks,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As very infants prattle of thy pride.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thou art a most pernicious usurer,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Froward by nature, enemy to peace,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Lascivious, wanton, more than well beseems

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: A man of thy profession and degree.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And for thy treachery, what’s more manifest,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: In that thou laid’st a trap to take my life

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As well at London Bridge as at the Tower?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Besides, I fear me, if thy thoughts were sifted,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The King, thy sovereign, is not quite exempt

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: From envious malice of thy swelling heart.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Gloucester, I do defy thee.—Lords, vouchsafe

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: To give me hearing what I shall reply.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: If I were covetous, ambitious, or perverse,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: As he will have me, how am I so poor?

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Or how haps it I seek not to advance

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Or raise myself, but keep my wonted calling?

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And for dissension, who preferreth peace

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: More than I do, except I be provoked?

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: No, my good lords, it is not that offends;

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: It is not that that hath incensed the Duke.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: It is because no one should sway but he,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: No one but he should be about the King;

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And that engenders thunder in his breast

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And makes him roar these accusations forth.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: But he shall know I am as good—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As good!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thou bastard of my grandfather!

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Ay, lordly sir; for what are you, I pray,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: But one imperious in another’s throne?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Am I not Protector, saucy priest?

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And am not I a prelate of the Church?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Yes, as an outlaw in a castle keeps,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And useth it to patronage his theft.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Unreverent Gloucester!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thou art reverend

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Touching thy spiritual function, not thy life.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Rome shall remedy this.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Roam thither then.

speaker: WARWICK
text: My lord, it were your duty to forbear.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Ay, so the Bishop be not overborne.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Methinks my lord should be religious,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And know the office that belongs to such.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Methinks his Lordship should be humbler.

speaker: WARWICK
text: It fitteth not a prelate so to plead.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Yes, when his holy state is touched so near.

speaker: WARWICK
text: State holy, or unhallowed, what of that?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Is not his Grace Protector to the King?

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Plantagenet, I see, must hold his tongue,

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Lest it be said “Speak, sirrah, when you should;

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Must your bold verdict enter talk with lords?”

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Else would I have a fling at Winchester.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Uncles of Gloucester and of Winchester,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The special watchmen of our English weal,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I would prevail, if prayers might prevail,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To join your hearts in love and amity.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, what a scandal is it to our crown

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That two such noble peers as you should jar!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Believe me, lords, my tender years can tell

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Civil dissension is a viperous worm

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That gnaws the bowels of the commonwealth.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What tumult ’s this?

speaker: WARWICK
text: An uproar, I dare warrant,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Begun through malice of the Bishop’s men.

speaker: MAYOR
text: O, my good lords, and virtuous Henry,

speaker: MAYOR
text: Pity the city of London, pity us!

speaker: MAYOR
text: The Bishop and the Duke of Gloucester’s men,

speaker: MAYOR
text: Forbidden late to carry any weapon,

speaker: MAYOR
text: Have filled their pockets full of pebble stones

speaker: MAYOR
text: And, banding themselves in contrary parts,

speaker: MAYOR
text: Do pelt so fast at one another’s pate

speaker: MAYOR
text: That many have their giddy brains knocked out;

speaker: MAYOR
text: Our windows are broke down in every street,

speaker: MAYOR
text: And we, for fear, compelled to shut our shops.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We charge you, on allegiance to ourself,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To hold your slaught’ring hands and keep the peace.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Pray, Uncle Gloucester, mitigate this strife.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Nay, if we be forbidden stones, we’ll

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: fall to it with our teeth.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Do what you dare, we are as resolute.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: You of my household, leave this peevish broil,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And set this unaccustomed fight aside.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: My lord, we know your Grace to be a man

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Just and upright, and, for your royal birth,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Inferior to none but to his Majesty;

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And ere that we will suffer such a prince,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: So kind a father of the commonweal,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: To be disgracèd by an inkhorn mate,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: We and our wives and children all will fight

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And have our bodies slaughtered by thy foes.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Ay, and the very parings of our nails

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Shall pitch a field when we are dead.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Stay, stay, I say!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And if you love me, as you say you do,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Let me persuade you to forbear awhile.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, how this discord doth afflict my soul!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Can you, my Lord of Winchester, behold

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My sighs and tears, and will not once relent?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Who should be pitiful if you be not?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Or who should study to prefer a peace

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If holy churchmen take delight in broils?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Yield, my Lord Protector—yield, Winchester—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Except you mean with obstinate repulse

speaker: WARWICK
text: To slay your sovereign and destroy the realm.

speaker: WARWICK
text: You see what mischief, and what murder too,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Hath been enacted through your enmity.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then be at peace, except you thirst for blood.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: He shall submit, or I will never yield.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Compassion on the King commands me stoop,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Or I would see his heart out ere the priest

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Should ever get that privilege of me.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Behold, my Lord of Winchester, the Duke

speaker: WARWICK
text: Hath banished moody discontented fury,

speaker: WARWICK
text: As by his smoothèd brows it doth appear.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why look you still so stern and tragical?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Here, Winchester, I offer thee my hand.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Fie, Uncle Beaufort! I have heard you preach

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That malice was a great and grievous sin;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And will not you maintain the thing you teach,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But prove a chief offender in the same?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Sweet king! The Bishop hath a kindly gird.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: For shame, my Lord of Winchester, relent;

speaker: WARWICK
text: What, shall a child instruct you what to do?

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Well, Duke of Gloucester, I will yield to thee;

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Love for thy love and hand for hand I give.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ay, but I fear me with a hollow heart.—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: See here, my friends and loving countrymen,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: This token serveth for a flag of truce

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Betwixt ourselves and all our followers,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: So help me God, as I dissemble not.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: So help me God, as I intend it not.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O, loving uncle—kind Duke of Gloucester—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How joyful am I made by this contract.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                Servingmen
                .
                 
              Away, my masters, trouble us no more,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But join in friendship as your lords have done.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Content. I’ll to the surgeon’s.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And so will I.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: And I will see what physic the tavern

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: affords.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Accept this scroll, most gracious sovereign,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Which in the right of Richard Plantagenet

speaker: WARWICK
text: We do exhibit to your Majesty.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Well urged, my Lord of Warwick.—For, sweet prince,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: An if your Grace mark every circumstance,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: You have great reason to do Richard right,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Especially for those occasions

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: At Eltham Place I told your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And those occasions, uncle, were of force.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Therefore, my loving lords, our pleasure is

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That Richard be restorèd to his blood.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Let Richard be restorèd to his blood;

speaker: WARWICK
text: So shall his father’s wrongs be recompensed.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: As will the rest, so willeth Winchester.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If Richard will be true, not that alone

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But all the whole inheritance I give

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That doth belong unto the house of York,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: From whence you spring by lineal descent.

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: Thy humble servant vows obedience

speaker: PLANTAGENET
text: And humble service till the point of death.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Stoop then, and set your knee against my foot;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And in reguerdon of that duty done

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I girt thee with the valiant sword of York.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Rise, Richard, like a true Plantagenet,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And rise created princely Duke of York.

speaker: YORK
text: And so thrive Richard as thy foes may fall!

speaker: YORK
text: And as my duty springs, so perish they

speaker: YORK
text: That grudge one thought against your Majesty.

speaker: ALL
text: Welcome, high prince, the mighty Duke of York.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Perish, base prince, ignoble Duke of York.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Now will it best avail your Majesty

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To cross the seas and to be crowned in France.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The presence of a king engenders love

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Amongst his subjects and his loyal friends,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As it disanimates his enemies.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When Gloucester says the word, King Henry goes,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For friendly counsel cuts off many foes.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Your ships already are in readiness.

speaker: EXETER
text: Ay, we may march in England or in France,

speaker: EXETER
text: Not seeing what is likely to ensue.

speaker: EXETER
text: This late dissension grown betwixt the peers

speaker: EXETER
text: Burns under feignèd ashes of forged love

speaker: EXETER
text: And will at last break out into a flame.

speaker: EXETER
text: As festered members rot but by degree

speaker: EXETER
text: Till bones and flesh and sinews fall away,

speaker: EXETER
text: So will this base and envious discord breed.

speaker: EXETER
text: And now I fear that fatal prophecy

speaker: EXETER
text: Which in the time of Henry named the Fifth

speaker: EXETER
text: Was in the mouth of every sucking babe:

speaker: EXETER
text: That Henry born at Monmouth should win all,

speaker: EXETER
text: And Henry born at Windsor should lose all,

speaker: EXETER
text: Which is so plain that Exeter doth wish

speaker: EXETER
text: His days may finish ere that hapless time.
